guide.boardTourTitle: Read the table before it breaks
guide.boardTourDetail: Each panel explains what a board component means, how to use it, and what danger signals to monitor.
guide.playerGuideDetail: Use this as the fast operational guide for the current ruleset.
guide.victoryModes1: Finish Resolution with every region at 1 Extraction Token or less.
guide.victoryModes2: Complete all active Beacons before sudden death closes the window.
guide.victoryModes3: In room play, public victory still fails if any Secret Mandate fails.
guide.victoryModes4: Local play removes Secret Mandates so every seat can plan in the open.
guide.victoryModes5: Some scenarios add victory gates (minimum round, required action, or progress threshold) before public victory can trigger.
guide.defeatChecks1: If any region reaches 6 Extraction Tokens, the coalition loses immediately.
guide.defeatChecks2: If any seat is reduced to 0 Comrades, the coalition loses immediately.
guide.defeatChecks3: If sudden death is reached without Liberation or Symbolic completion, the coalition loses.
guide.defeatChecks4: In room play, Secret Mandates are checked at victory; failed mandates convert public victory into defeat.
guide.systemPressureFlow1: System Phase resolves Crisis pressure, then active System escalations, then intervention effects.
guide.systemPressureFlow2: Global Gaze and War Machine change campaign math and escalation tempo, not just flavor text.
guide.systemPressureFlow3: Extraction Tokens are structural pressure; each round asks where the system is hardening fastest.
guide.loop1: System Phase: resolve Crisis pressure, active System escalations, and interventions before the coalition can answer.
guide.loop2: Coalition Phase: each seat prepares two moves, then all seats mark ready.
guide.loop3: Resolution Phase: prepared moves resolve by priority, then the table checks victory, defeat, and mandate fallout.
guide.loop4: Launch Campaign always resolves as 2d6 with target 8+, then modifiers and outcomes apply.
guide.loop5: Scenario-specific victory gates can require a minimum round, a named action trigger, or gameplay progress before victory checks apply.
guide.coordinationTension1: Cooperate on public survival: stop 6 Extraction breaches, protect Comrades, and keep pressure distributed.
guide.coordinationTension2: Protect private lines at the same time: Secret Mandates can still collapse a public win in room play.
guide.coordinationTension3: Queue order is strategy: place stabilizing actions first when you expect backlash or intervention.
guide.boardReading1: Scan regions at 4-5 Extraction first: those are immediate breach candidates next round.
guide.boardReading2: Track Global Gaze and War Machine together; they forecast campaign risk and escalation pace.
guide.boardReading3: Use Evidence where it shifts structural fronts, not only where it produces short-term relief.
guide.boardReading4: Defense is temporary. Treat it as a timing shield, not permanent safety.

mapRegions.title: Board Map and Regions
mapRegions.what: The map is the shared field of pressure. Every region carries Extraction Tokens, Defense, and Comrades.
mapRegions.how: Inspect regions before committing actions so the table sees where pressure is concentrating.
mapRegions.watch: If multiple regions trend toward 5 Extraction Tokens together, shift to stabilization.

extractionTokens.title: Extraction Tokens
extractionTokens.what: Extraction Tokens show where the System has hardened control.
extractionTokens.how: Remove them with Launch Campaign, Build Solidarity, and resistance effects before compounding.
extractionTokens.watch: Any region at 6 Extraction Tokens is immediate defeat.

globalGaze.title: Global Gaze
globalGaze.what: Global Gaze tracks witness and attention pressure.
globalGaze.how: Protect it while campaigning so setbacks do not seal the round against the coalition.
globalGaze.watch: A collapsing Global Gaze can accelerate structural pressure.

warMachine.title: War Machine
warMachine.what: War Machine tracks militarized escalation capacity.
warMachine.how: Factor it into campaign timing before committing Comrades and Evidence.
warMachine.watch: High War Machine raises round danger and escalation tempo.

domains.title: Domain Track Bar
domains.what: Domains represent structural fronts under pressure.
domains.how: Target domains that align with immediate map risk and scenario obligations.
domains.watch: Neglected domains rebound through cards and vulnerabilities.

playerStrip.title: Player Strip
playerStrip.what: The strip shows each seat's Comrades, Evidence, and readiness.
playerStrip.how: Use it to check who can pay costs and who is overextended.
playerStrip.watch: Any seat at 0 Comrades means immediate defeat.

actionDock.title: Action Dock and Queue Priority
actionDock.what: Seats prepare and order their actions here.
actionDock.how: Place stabilization ahead of risky campaigns when pressure is high.
actionDock.watch: Queue-order mistakes can expose regions before protection lands.

decks.title: Crisis, System, Resistance Deck Rail
decks.what: The rail reveals current pressure and available movement tools.
decks.how: Read deck reveals before finalizing actions.
decks.watch: Persistent System escalations can stack into attrition.

beacons.title: Beacons and Symbolic Pressure
beacons.what: In Symbolic mode, active Beacons define public breakthrough requirements.
beacons.how: Coordinate cross-seat actions around beacon conditions without abandoning extraction control.
beacons.watch: Beacon progress without board stability often collapses.

secretMandates.title: Secret Mandates
secretMandates.what: Secret Mandates are private obligations in room play.
secretMandates.how: Coordinate publicly while preserving each seat's mandate condition.
secretMandates.watch: Public victory fails in room play if any Secret Mandate fails.

terminalWarnings.title: Terminal Warning Patterns
terminalWarnings.what: Collapse signals usually appear together: breach risk, seat exhaustion, and mandate instability.
terminalWarnings.how: Run a quick warning checklist before ending each round.
terminalWarnings.watch: When two warning signals align, shift immediately to stabilization.

guide.boardTourMapRegionsWhat: The map is the shared field of pressure. Regions hold Extraction Tokens, Defense, and Comrades at the same time.
guide.boardTourMapRegionsHow: Select regions to read vulnerabilities and current token counts before committing moves.
guide.boardTourMapRegionsWatch: Watch clusters where multiple regions approach 5 Extraction Tokens together.
guide.boardTourExtractionTokensWhat: Extraction Tokens represent where the System is tightening material control.
guide.boardTourExtractionTokensHow: Remove them through Launch Campaign, Build Solidarity, and card effects before they compound.
guide.boardTourExtractionTokensWatch: Any region reaching 6 Extraction Tokens is immediate defeat.
guide.boardTourGlobalGazeWhat: Global Gaze tracks public attention and witness capacity across the board.
guide.boardTourGlobalGazeHow: Raise it through testimony and successful pressure shifts to improve campaign conditions.
guide.boardTourGlobalGazeWatch: Collapsing Global Gaze can accelerate structural escalations and campaign setbacks.
guide.boardTourWarMachineWhat: War Machine tracks the System's militarized response capacity.
guide.boardTourWarMachineHow: Plan with War Machine in mind before committing costly campaigns.
guide.boardTourWarMachineWatch: High War Machine raises risk, hardens rounds, and can trigger escalation pressure.
guide.boardTourDomainsWhat: Domains track structural fronts under pressure: war, hunger, truth, and more.
guide.boardTourDomainsHow: Target domains with campaigns and solidarity to open systemic breathing room.
guide.boardTourDomainsWatch: Ignore a domain too long and it rebounds through cards and vulnerabilities.
guide.boardTourPlayerStripWhat: The player strip shows each seat's Comrades, Evidence, and prepared moves.
guide.boardTourPlayerStripHow: Use it to confirm who can pay costs and who is overextended this round.
guide.boardTourPlayerStripWatch: Any seat at 0 Comrades triggers immediate defeat.
guide.boardTourActionDockWhat: The action dock is where seats prepare two moves and lock order.
guide.boardTourActionDockHow: Place defensive and setup actions before high-risk campaigns when pressure is high.
guide.boardTourActionDockWatch: Priority mistakes can waste Evidence and expose regions before protection lands.
guide.boardTourDecksWhat: The deck rail stages Crisis draws, persistent System escalations, and Resistance tools.
guide.boardTourDecksHow: Read revealed cards before committing: they define what this round is asking from the table.
guide.boardTourDecksWatch: Stacked escalations can outpace recovery if ignored for multiple rounds.
guide.boardTourBeaconsWhat: In Symbolic mode, active Beacons define the coalition's public breakthrough path.
guide.boardTourBeaconsHow: Track beacon conditions each round and align actions across seats to complete all active Beacons.
guide.boardTourBeaconsWatch: Symbolic progress that ignores extraction often collapses before completion.
guide.boardTourSecretMandatesWhat: Secret Mandates are private movement obligations in room play.
guide.boardTourSecretMandatesHow: Coordinate publicly while quietly preserving each seat's mandate condition.
guide.boardTourSecretMandatesWatch: Public victory still fails if any Secret Mandate fails in room play.
guide.boardTourTerminalWarningsWhat: Terminal warnings are recurring board patterns that precede collapse.
guide.boardTourTerminalWarningsHow: Each round, check: region at 5+, seat near 0 Comrades, mandate risk, and sudden death proximity.
guide.boardTourTerminalWarningsWatch: If two or more warnings appear together, shift to stabilization over expansion.
guide.beacon1: Symbolic mode reveals three active Beacons from a six-card deck.
guide.beacon2: Online rooms keep Secret Mandates private. Reaching the public win condition while any mandate fails still ends in defeat there.
guide.beacon3: Local tables remove Secret Mandates entirely so every movement can coordinate in the open.

game.modeDescLiberation: In Liberation mode, the coalition must force extraction down in every region without breaking its own movements in the process.
game.modeDescSymbolic: In Symbolic mode, the coalition must align the active Beacons before the symbolic window closes, while still holding back extraction.

campaignResult.titleBacklash: The campaign meets the System’s reprisal.
campaignResult.titleAttention: The campaign falters, but the struggle is seen.
campaignResult.titleSuccess: The campaign breaks open a line of advance.

campaignResult.descriptionBacklash: The threshold was not reached. The movement pays a price, yet the struggle continues beyond this blow.
campaignResult.descriptionAttention: The threshold was missed. Public attention shifts, but extraction remains in place and the movement regathers.
campaignResult.descriptionSuccess: The threshold was reached. The movement secures real ground, though never without cost.

stones_cry_out.name: Where the Stones Cry Out
stones_cry_out.description: A six-region movement struggle against extraction, militarism, hunger, and erasure under systemic pressure.
stones_cry_out.introduction: The coalition is not trying to survive a bad night. It is trying to hold six fronts at once: reduce Extraction Tokens, move Evidence across borders, and keep every movement's Secret Mandate alive long enough to win together.

tahrir_square.name: 2011 — TAHRIR SQUARE
tahrir_square.description: "For eighteen days, we were free. Then the counterrevolution reorganized."
tahrir_square.introduction: Lead the January 25 Revolution as a plural movement fighting through regime collapse, military transition, and re-consolidation. The System shifts shape, but it does not disappear.

woman_life_freedom.name: 2022 — WOMAN, LIFE, FREEDOM
woman_life_freedom.description: "Woman, Life, Freedom" against patriarchal rule and militarized repression.
woman_life_freedom.introduction: After the morality police killed Jina (Mahsa) Amini, an uprising spread across Iran. Women and schoolgirls moved at the front while the System answered with surveillance, detention, and force.

algerian_war_of_independence.name: 1954 — ALGERIAN WAR OF INDEPENDENCE
algerian_war_of_independence.description: "Liberation is necessary. Its cost is permanent."
algerian_war_of_independence.introduction: Algerian movements confront a colonial system of extraction, militarism, detention, and settler veto. The struggle runs from Algiers to Kabylia, the Saharan south, the Tunisian frontier, and the metropole itself.

egypt_1919_revolution.name: 1919 — EGYPT RISES
egypt_1919_revolution.introduction: The 1919 uprising is a mass coalition struggle. Students, railway workers, and women’s Action Circles must hold Cairo and the provinces while British occupation attempts to suppress popular mobilization through detention and martial law.

when_the_corridors_burn.name: 2026 — WHEN THE CORRIDORS BURN
when_the_corridors_burn.description: A corridor war scenario where bombardment, sanctions, shipping, and basing form one connected system.
when_the_corridors_burn.introduction: March 2026 is not one war. It is a corridor condition stretching from Gaza and south Lebanon through Hormuz and Suez to Havana, Caracas, and the Black Sea.

Amazon.strapline: The forest is treated as inventory.

Sahel.name: The Sahel
Sahel.description: Climate breakdown, hunger, border regimes, and securitized aid.
Sahel.strapline: Security doctrine feeds the famine line.

Mekong.name: The Mekong
Mekong.description: Water memory against dams, agribusiness, and fossil corridors.
Mekong.strapline: The river is partitioned for profit.

Andes.name: The Andes
Andes.description: Highland resistance to debt, lithium extraction, and privatized life.
Andes.strapline: The mountain is priced before it is heard.

Cairo.name: Cairo
Cairo.description: The center of power, Tahrir Square, the Ministry of Interior.

Alexandria.name: Alexandria
Alexandria.description: Industrial hubs, Khaled Said’s neighborhood, dockworker resistance.

NileDelta.name: Nile Delta
NileDelta.description: Textile strikes, agricultural density, brutal state security presence.

UpperEgypt.name: Upper Egypt
UpperEgypt.description: Traditional strongholds, sectarian flashpoints, economic neglect.

Suez.name: Suez
Suez.description: Militarized zones, early uprisings, intense crackdown.

Sinai.name: Sinai
Sinai.description: Bedouin marginalization, security vacuums, border violence.

DeltaRailCorridor.name: Delta Rail Corridor
DeltaRailCorridor.description: The vital infrastructure link where railway workers sabotage colonial movement.

CanalZone.name: Canal Zone
CanalZone.description: British military strongholds and global transport artery under siege by local labor.

RuralVillages.name: Rural Villages
RuralVillages.description: Fellahin networks organizing against crop seizure and punitive colonial levies.

Kurdistan.name: Kurdistan
Kurdistan.description: Jina Amini's home, fierce resistance, heavy militarization.

Isfahan.name: Isfahan
Isfahan.description: Historic city, water protests, schoolgirl resistance.

Khuzestan.name: Khuzestan
Khuzestan.description: Oil rich, ethnically marginalized, water crises.

Algiers.name: Algiers
Algiers.description: The capital corridor from Alger through Blida and Tipaza, where urban insurgency meets colonial administration.

KabylieMountains.name: Kabylie Mountains
KabylieMountains.description: Kabylia and its mountain approaches, a guerrilla stronghold for endurance, shelter, and political cohesion.

Oran.name: Oran
Oran.description: The western settler coast around Oran, where colonial power hardens into reaction and veto.
Oran.strapline: The settler coast refuses to yield.

SaharaSouth.name: Sahara South
SaharaSouth.description: The Saharan south, where long distances, desert routes, and reprisal test rural organizing.

GazaWestBank.name: Gaza-West Bank
GazaWestBank.description: Siege, bombardment, displacement, prison raids, and survival organizing from Gaza to the fragmented West Bank.
GazaWestBank.strapline: The moral center bleeds under managed erasure.

LebanonNorthernFront.name: Lebanon-Northern Front
LebanonNorthernFront.description: Cross-border escalation, displacement, and the risk that northern war becomes the pretext for wider regional rupture.
LebanonNorthernFront.strapline: The frontier expands whenever the war-state needs another horizon.

GulfHormuzCorridor.name: Gulf-Hormuz Corridor
GulfHormuzCorridor.description: Basing states, insurance markets, oil logistics, and retaliatory fire along the narrowest energy corridor in the theatre.
GulfHormuzCorridor.strapline: The shipping lane is also a trigger line.

RedSeaSuezCorridor.name: Red Sea-Suez Corridor
RedSeaSuezCorridor.description: Port labor, Suez choke pressure, Yemeni retaliation, and the supply chain that keeps war materially alive.
RedSeaSuezCorridor.strapline: Every container route hides a battlefield.

CaribbeanSiege.name: Caribbean Siege
CaribbeanSiege.description: Pressure on Cuba and Venezuela through sanctions, banking restrictions, oil coercion, platform narratives, and Monroe-style threats.
CaribbeanSiege.strapline: The blockade line moves through ports, banks, and screens.

BlackSeaEasternFurnace.name: Black Sea-Eastern Furnace
BlackSeaEasternFurnace.description: Russia's continuing war on Ukraine, grain disruption, and the way overextension abroad still feeds the Atlantic war tempo.
BlackSeaEasternFurnace.strapline: Another war keeps feeding this one.

TunisianBorder.name: Tunisian Border
TunisianBorder.description: The eastern frontier toward Tunisia, a corridor for supplies, testimony, and strategic passage.
TunisianBorder.strapline: The corridor that keeps the struggle breathing.

FrenchMetropoleInfluence.name: French Metropole Influence
FrenchMetropoleInfluence.description: Political and media struggle inside France over legitimacy, repression, and the terms of colonial exit.
FrenchMetropoleInfluence.strapline: The colonial center is part of the battlefield.

student_committees.name: Student Committees
student_committees.passive: Investigate adds +1 Evidence in Cairo and campaigns for Public Testimony gain +1.
student_committees.weakness: Mass arrests in Cairo cost 1 extra Comrade when War Machine is 6 or higher.
student_committees.mandateDescription: End with Public Testimony at 6 or more and Cairo at 2 or fewer Extraction Tokens.

railway_workers.name: Railway Workers
railway_workers.passive: Campaigns for Strike Capacity gain +1 and actions in Delta Rail Corridor cost 1 fewer Comrade.
railway_workers.weakness: Railway lockouts set 1 less Defense in the Corridor.
railway_workers.mandateDescription: End with Strike Capacity at 5 or more and Delta Rail Corridor at 1 or fewer Extraction Tokens.

womens_action_circles.name: Women’s Action Circles
womens_action_circles.passive: +1 to all National Uprising campaigns and Organizing in Cairo/Alexandria adds +1 Comrade.
womens_action_circles.weakness: Repression of public demonstrations reduces National Uprising by 1.
womens_action_circles.mandateDescription: End with National Uprising at 7 or more and Alexandria at 2 or fewer Extraction Tokens.

provincial_organizers.name: Provincial Organizers
provincial_organizers.passive: Organize adds +1 Comrade in Upper Egypt/Rural Villages and outreach is free.
provincial_organizers.weakness: Military provincial reprisals remove 2 Comrades instead of 1.
provincial_organizers.mandateDescription: End with Mass Representation at 5 or more and Rural Villages at 1 or fewer Extraction Tokens.

congo_basin_collective.name: Congo Basin Collective
congo_basin_collective.passive: Organize gains +1 Comrade in Congo and Launch Campaign gains +1 there.
congo_basin_collective.mandateDescription: End with Congo at 2 or fewer Extraction Tokens and Dying Planet ahead of War Machine.

levant_sumud.name: Levant Sumud Front
levant_sumud.passive: Defend gains +1 Defense in the Levant and campaigns against War Machine gain +1 there.
levant_sumud.weakness: Organize outside the Levant gains -1 Comrade.
levant_sumud.mandateDescription: End with the Levant at 1 or fewer Extraction Tokens and War Machine at 6 or less.

mekong_echo_network.name: Mekong Echo Network
mekong_echo_network.passive: Investigate gains +1 Evidence in the Mekong and support cards gain +1 on Silenced Truth campaigns.
mekong_echo_network.weakness: Defend outside the Mekong sets 1 less Defense.
mekong_echo_network.mandateDescription: End with the Mekong at 1 or fewer Extraction Tokens and Silenced Truth at 5 or more.

amazon_guardians.name: Amazon Guardians
amazon_guardians.passive: Campaigns in the Amazon or Dying Planet gain +1 and Organize gains +1 Comrade in the Amazon.
amazon_guardians.weakness: Smuggle Evidence can move only 1 Evidence at a time.
amazon_guardians.mandateDescription: End with the Amazon at 1 or fewer Extraction Tokens and Fossil Grip at 5 or more progress.

april_6_youth.name: April 6 Youth Movement
april_6_youth.passive: The Square: If Cairo has 5+ Comrades, actions there gain +2. Martyrdom: If Comrades lost in Cairo, Revolutionary Wave +1.
april_6_youth.mandateDescription: End with Revolutionary Wave at 8 or more.

labor_movement.name: Independent Labor Unions
labor_movement.passive: Labor Strike actions gain +2 on success.
labor_movement.weakness: Campaigns outside industrial zones cost +1 Comrade.
labor_movement.mandateDescription: End with Empty Stomach at 5 or more.

independent_journalists.name: Independent Journalists
independent_journalists.weakness: Defend outside Cairo sets 1 less Defense.
independent_journalists.mandateDescription: End with Silenced Truth at 6 or more.

rights_defenders.name: Rights Defenders
rights_defenders.mandateDescription: End with War Machine at 4 or less.

kurdish_women.name: Kurdish Women
kurdish_women.passive: Actions cost 1 less Comrade in Kurdistan.
kurdish_women.mandateDescription: End with Patriarchal Grip < 5 and Kurdistan at 1 or fewer Extraction Tokens.

student_union.name: University Student Union
student_union.passive: +1 to all Digital Front (Silenced Truth) campaigns.

male_allies.name: Male Allies
male_allies.passive: May transfer actions to a female faction without spending Evidence.
male_allies.weakness: Cannot resolve Burn Veil action directly.

fln_urban_cells.name: FLN Urban Cells
fln_urban_cells.passive: Investigate gains +1 Evidence in Algiers, and urban cells turn the capital corridor into launch infrastructure.
fln_urban_cells.weakness: Failed campaigns in Algiers immediately add 1 Extraction there.
fln_urban_cells.mandateDescription: End with Global Gaze at 12 or more and Algiers at 2 or fewer Extraction Tokens.

kabyle_maquis.name: Kabyle Maquis
kabyle_maquis.passive: Launch Campaign gains +1 in Kabylie Mountains, and mountain offensives strike harder from the maquis.
kabyle_maquis.weakness: Global Appeal costs +1 Evidence when War Machine is 8 or higher.
kabyle_maquis.mandateDescription: End with Liberation Cohesion at 6 or more and Kabylie Mountains at 1 or fewer Extraction Tokens.

rural_organizing_committees.name: Rural Organizing Committees
rural_organizing_committees.passive: Organize gains +1 Comrade in Sahara South, and solidarity is cheaper across rural routes there.
rural_organizing_committees.weakness: Military intervention in Sahara South removes 1 extra Comrade.
rural_organizing_committees.mandateDescription: End with Repression Cycle at 6 or less and Sahara South at 2 or fewer Extraction Tokens.

border_solidarity_networks.name: Border Solidarity Networks
border_solidarity_networks.passive: Smuggle Evidence can move up to 2 Evidence, and cross-border supply gains +1 Comrade.
border_solidarity_networks.weakness: Border Closure effects hit this seat first when multiple seats are valid.
border_solidarity_networks.mandateDescription: End with Torture & Detention Network at 4 or less and French Metropole Influence at 2 or fewer Extraction Tokens.

palestinian_sumud_committees.name: Palestinian Sumud Committees
palestinian_sumud_committees.passive: Organize gains +2 Comrades in Gaza-West Bank and Launch Campaign gains +1 there.
palestinian_sumud_committees.mandateDescription: End with Gaza-West Bank at 2 or fewer Extraction Tokens, Witness Corridors at 4 or more, and Egyptian Corridor Posture at 3 or more.

gaza_west_bank_witness_medics.name: Gaza / West Bank Witness and Medics Network
gaza_west_bank_witness_medics.passive: Investigate gains +1 Evidence in Gaza-West Bank and campaigns on Witness Corridors gain +1.
gaza_west_bank_witness_medics.weakness: Defend actions gain no home-region bonus.
gaza_west_bank_witness_medics.mandateDescription: End with Witness Corridors at 5 or more, Gaza-West Bank at 2 or fewer Extraction Tokens, and Global Gaze at 10 or more.

venezuelan_communal_councils.name: Venezuelan Communal Councils
venezuelan_communal_councils.passive: Organize gains +2 Comrades in Caribbean Siege and campaigns on Bread Under Siege gain +1 there.
venezuelan_communal_councils.mandateDescription: End with Caribbean Siege at 2 or fewer Extraction Tokens, Bread Under Siege at 4 or more, and Saudi Posture at least Hedge.

cuban_cdr_neighborhood_defense.name: Cuban CDR and Neighborhood Defense Networks
cuban_cdr_neighborhood_defense.passive: Investigate gains +1 Evidence in Caribbean Siege and Defend gains +1 there.
cuban_cdr_neighborhood_defense.weakness: Organize outside Caribbean Siege gains no home bonus.
cuban_cdr_neighborhood_defense.mandateDescription: End with Witness Corridors at 3 or more, Sanctions Breach at 3 or more, and Caribbean Siege at 2 or fewer Extraction Tokens.

corridor_workers_refuge_networks.name: Corridor Workers and Refuge Networks
corridor_workers_refuge_networks.passive: Investigate gains +1 Evidence in Red Sea-Suez Corridor and campaigns on Chokepoint Breakage gain +1.
corridor_workers_refuge_networks.weakness: Global Appeal costs +1 Evidence when Gulf Blowback is 4 or higher.
corridor_workers_refuge_networks.mandateTitle: Open the Passage, Not the War
corridor_workers_refuge_networks.mandateDescription: End with Red Sea-Suez Corridor at 2 or fewer Extraction Tokens, Gulf-Hormuz Corridor at 2 or fewer, and Chokepoint Breakage at 4 or more.

organize.name: Organize
organize.description: Roll 1d6 Comrades into a region, plus pressure bonuses where extraction is already entrenched.

investigate.name: Investigate
investigate.description: Generate Evidence and draw resistance cards.

launch_campaign.name: Launch Campaign
launch_campaign.description: Commit Comrades and Evidence to a 2d6 campaign against a Domain in a region.

build_solidarity.name: Build Solidarity
build_solidarity.description: Spend 3 Comrades in-region to advance a Domain without a roll.

smuggle_evidence.name: Smuggle Evidence
smuggle_evidence.description: Move Evidence to another faction through a risky corridor.

international_outreach.name: Global Appeal
international_outreach.description: Spend Evidence to raise Global Gaze.

defend.name: Defend
defend.description: Convert Comrades into a one-round Defense Rating against the next intervention.

play_card.name: Play Card
play_card.description: Resolve an action card from hand for direct tactical impact.

go_viral.name: Go Viral
go_viral.description: Spend 1 Evidence, raise Global Gaze by 1.

burn_veil.name: Burn Veil
burn_veil.description: Raise Global Gaze by 2, lose 1 Comrade.

schoolgirl_network.name: Schoolgirl Network
schoolgirl_network.description: Gain 1 Evidence, next action in region costs 1 less.

compose_chant.name: Compose Chant
compose_chant.description: Spend 1 Evidence, create permanent +1 track modifier in region.

coordinate_digital.name: Digital Coordination
coordinate_digital.description: Spend 1 Evidence, open the table-wide re-plan window.

expose_regime_lies.name: Expose Regime Lies
expose_regime_lies.description: Spend 2 Evidence, reduce War Machine by 1.

beacon_levant_corridor.title: Corridor Of Return
beacon_levant_corridor.description: Levant at 1 or fewer Extraction Tokens and Global Gaze at 10 or more.

beacon_mekong_testimony.title: River Testimony
beacon_mekong_testimony.description: Mekong at 1 or fewer Extraction Tokens and Silenced Truth at 5 or more.

beacon_amazon_sovereignty.title: Forest Sovereignty
beacon_amazon_sovereignty.description: Amazon at 1 or fewer Extraction Tokens and Dying Planet at 6 or more.

beacon_congo_commons.title: Copper Commons
beacon_congo_commons.description: Congo at 1 or fewer Extraction Tokens and Fossil Grip at 5 or more.

beacon_sahel_bread_pact.title: Bread Pact
beacon_sahel_bread_pact.description: Sahel at 1 or fewer Extraction Tokens and Empty Stomach at 5 or more.

beacon_andes_charter.title: Mountain Charter
beacon_andes_charter.description: Andes at 1 or fewer Extraction Tokens and Gilded Cage at 5 or more.

beacon_tahrir_18_days.title: The 18 Days
beacon_tahrir_18_days.description: Cairo at 2 or fewer Extraction Tokens (Tahrir never empty).

beacon_tahrir_labor_student.title: Labor-Student Alliance
beacon_tahrir_labor_student.description: Successfully call 3 Labor Strikes (Represented by high Empty Stomach domain).

beacon_wlf_global_solidarity.title: Global Solidarity
beacon_wlf_global_solidarity.description: Global Gaze at least 12 and Patriarchal Grip at most 4.

beacon_wlf_no_executions.title: Halt Executions
beacon_wlf_no_executions.description: War Machine drops to 3 or lower.

beacon_tahrir_victory.title: Tahrir Victory
beacon_tahrir_victory.description: Cairo at 1 or fewer Extraction and Revolutionary Wave at 8 or more.

beacon_labor_solidarity.title: Labor Solidarity
beacon_labor_solidarity.description: Industrial hubs at 1 or fewer Extraction and Empty Stomach at 5 or more.

beacon_wlf_liberation.title: WLF Liberation
beacon_wlf_liberation.description: Kurdistan and Tehran at 1 or fewer Extraction and Patriarchal Grip at 3 or less.

beacon_digital_truth.title: Digital Truth
beacon_digital_truth.description: 5 regions at 1 or fewer Extraction and Silenced Truth at 8 or more.

beacon_alg_torture_exposed.title: Expose the Torture Network
beacon_alg_torture_exposed.description: The torture system is forced into the open.

beacon_alg_tribunal_acknowledgement.title: Force International Reckoning
beacon_alg_tribunal_acknowledgement.description: An international tribunal acknowledges colonial abuses.

beacon_egy_student_strike.title: March 9: The Student Strike
beacon_egy_student_strike.description: Students at Al-Azhar and schools across Cairo trigger the mass uprising.

beacon_egy_railway_sabotage.title: Delta Rail Blockade
beacon_egy_railway_sabotage.description: Railway lines are severed, preventing British military reinforcements.

beacon_egy_womens_march.title: March 16: Women’s Demonstration
beacon_egy_womens_march.description: Women take the lead in public defiance, shattering gendered colonial constraints.

beacon_egy_funerary_uprising.title: The Funerary Uprising
beacon_egy_funerary_uprising.description: Funeral processions for fallen comrades become massive political mobilizations.

beacon_egy_alexandria_dock_strike.title: Alexandria Labor Solidarity
beacon_egy_alexandria_dock_strike.description: Dockworkers and factory unions halt the logistics of occupation.

beacon_egy_provincial_defection.title: Assiut Defiance
beacon_egy_provincial_defection.description: Major provincial centers declare liberation, forcing the British to retreat.

res_archive_leak.name: Archive Leak
res_archive_leak.text: Raise Global Gaze by 1 and gain 1 Evidence.

res_mutual_aid_convoy.name: Mutual Aid Convoy
res_mutual_aid_convoy.text: Remove 1 Extraction Token from the target region.

res_radio_uprising.name: Radio Uprising
res_radio_uprising.text: Advance Silenced Truth by 1 and add 1 Comrade to the target region.

res_strike_fund.name: Strike Fund
res_strike_fund.text: Add 3 Comrades to the target region.

res_lawyers_brief.name: Lawyers Brief
res_lawyers_brief.text: Advance Gilded Cage by 1 and reduce War Machine by 1.

res_tahrir_cell_phone_video.name: Cell Phone Video
res_tahrir_cell_phone_video.text: Gain 2 Evidence. This can also fuel Go Viral.

res_tahrir_al_jazeera_interview.name: Al Jazeera Interview
res_tahrir_al_jazeera_interview.text: Gain 3 Evidence and raise Global Gaze by 1.

res_tahrir_lawyer_brief.name: Lawyer's Legal Brief
res_tahrir_lawyer_brief.text: Add 1 Comrade in Alexandria and advance Patriarchal Grip by 1.

res_wlf_vpn_network.name: VPN Network
res_wlf_vpn_network.text: Gain 2 Evidence and push testimony past the blackout.

res_wlf_diaspora_protest.name: Diaspora Protest Rally
res_wlf_diaspora_protest.text: Add 2 Comrades in the chosen region.

crisis_military_raid.name: Military Raid
crisis_military_raid.text: A logistics corridor is secured through direct force.

crisis_media_smear.name: Media Smear
crisis_media_smear.text: The movement is reframed as disorder to cool public attention.

crisis_fuel_convoy.name: Fuel Convoy
crisis_fuel_convoy.text: The corridor reopens under armed protection.

crisis_border_lockdown.name: Border Lockdown
crisis_border_lockdown.text: Containment expands under the language of security.

crisis_hunger_discipline.name: Hunger Discipline
crisis_hunger_discipline.text: Debt, rationing, and coercion force communities back into dependence.

crisis_climate_shock.name: Climate Shock
crisis_climate_shock.text: Disaster is turned into a fast lane for seizure.

crisis_memory_purge.name: Memory Purge
crisis_memory_purge.text: Archives, schools, and ceremonies are treated as hostile terrain.

crisis_attention_backlash.name: Attention Backlash
crisis_attention_backlash.text: Visibility triggers a sharper retaliatory strike.

crisis_port_seizure.name: Port Seizure
crisis_port_seizure.text: Trade chokepoints are tightened to discipline the region.

crisis_platform_blackout.name: Platform Blackout
crisis_platform_blackout.text: The channel collapses under coordinated censorship.

sys_emergency_powers.name: Emergency Powers Enacted
sys_emergency_powers.text: Exception is written into ordinary rule. Campaigns face a higher bar from here forward.

sys_resource_privatization_wave.name: Resource Privatization Wave
sys_resource_privatization_wave.text: Every new crisis becomes another pretext to deepen the carve-up.

sys_militarized_infrastructure.name: Militarized Infrastructure
sys_militarized_infrastructure.text: Roads, ports, and corridors become permanent security architecture.

sys_surveillance_normalization.name: Surveillance Normalization
sys_surveillance_normalization.text: Every campaign now moves through denser fog and better targeting.

sys_structural_adjustment_program.name: Structural Adjustment Program
sys_structural_adjustment_program.text: Appeals to the outside now cost more because every concession is monetized.

sys_counterinsurgency_doctrine.name: Counterinsurgency Doctrine Expansion
sys_counterinsurgency_doctrine.text: Evidence circulates more slowly as suppression becomes doctrine.

crisis_tahrir_central_security.name: Central Security Forces
crisis_tahrir_central_security.text: Military intervention in Cairo. Add 2 Extraction to Cairo.

crisis_tahrir_internet_shutdown.name: Internet Shutdown
crisis_tahrir_internet_shutdown.text: The state cuts access. All players lose 1 Evidence.

crisis_tahrir_camel_battle.name: Camel Battle
crisis_tahrir_camel_battle.text: Regime thugs attack Tahrir on horses. Remove Comrades from Cairo. Gain Global Gaze.

crisis_tahrir_state_tv_lies.name: State TV Lies
crisis_tahrir_state_tv_lies.text: Regime claims protests are foreign agents. Reduce Global Gaze.

crisis_tahrir_military_trials.name: Military Trials
crisis_tahrir_military_trials.text: Protesters are tried in military courts. Remove Comrades, gain Evidence.

crisis_tahrir_constitutional_declaration.name: Constitutional Declaration
crisis_tahrir_constitutional_declaration.text: Presidency grants itself unlimited power. Add to Unfinished Justice.

crisis_tahrir_rabaa_massacre.name: Rabaa Massacre
crisis_tahrir_rabaa_massacre.text: A brutal crackdown. Gain permanent Global Gaze and add Extraction.

sys_tahrir_state_of_emergency.name: State of Emergency
sys_tahrir_state_of_emergency.text: The interior ministry locks down transit.

crisis_wlf_morality_police_raid.name: Morality Police Raid
crisis_wlf_morality_police_raid.text: A patrol hits Tehran. Remove Comrades and add Extraction.

crisis_wlf_internet_blackout.name: Internet Blackout
crisis_wlf_internet_blackout.text: The state cuts access. All players lose Evidence. Global Gaze drops.

crisis_wlf_gas_attacks_on_schools.name: Gas Attacks on Schools
crisis_wlf_gas_attacks_on_schools.text: Retaliation against schoolgirls. War Machine increases.

crisis_wlf_sham_trials_and_executions.name: Sham Trials and Executions
crisis_wlf_sham_trials_and_executions.text: Protesters are executed to instill fear. Patriarchal Grip +1.

crisis_wlf_irgc_deployment.name: IRGC Deployment
crisis_wlf_irgc_deployment.text: The Revolutionary Guard steps in. Add Extraction to peripheral regions.

sys_wlf_hijab_crackdown.name: Hijab Crackdown
sys_wlf_hijab_crackdown.text: The regime turns compulsory modesty law into a disciplined daily crackdown.

crs_alg_battle_of_algiers.name: Battle of Algiers
crs_alg_battle_of_algiers.text: The colonial state saturates the capital corridor with raids, checkpoints, and collective punishment.

crs_alg_oas_terror_attack.name: OAS Terror Attack
crs_alg_oas_terror_attack.text: Settler terror escalates to fracture civilian life and harden colonial resolve.

crs_alg_rural_village_burned.name: Rural Village Burned
crs_alg_rural_village_burned.text: Scorched-earth tactics punish the countryside and disrupt organizing.

crs_alg_mass_arrest_sweep.name: Mass Arrest Sweep
crs_alg_mass_arrest_sweep.text: Detention and disappearance reach deep into the movement.

crs_alg_french_national_assembly_debate.name: French National Assembly Debate
crs_alg_french_national_assembly_debate.text: The metropole debates reform while refusing to break the structure of colonial rule.

crs_alg_international_press_leak.name: International Press Leak
crs_alg_international_press_leak.text: Testimony crosses borders and weakens the colonial story.

crs_alg_evian_talks.name: Evian Talks
crs_alg_evian_talks.text: Negotiations open without ending coercion across the front.

crs_alg_border_closure.name: Border Closure
crs_alg_border_closure.text: Supply corridors tighten under coordinated surveillance.

crs_alg_settler_militias_mobilize.name: Settler Militias Mobilize
crs_alg_settler_militias_mobilize.text: Pied-noir forces organize to sabotage decolonization and punish civilians.

crs_alg_torture_network_exposed.name: Torture Network Exposed
crs_alg_torture_network_exposed.text: The machinery of torture becomes undeniable abroad.

crs_alg_political_assassination.name: Political Assassination
crs_alg_political_assassination.text: Elimination of movement leadership strains unity and trust.

crs_alg_referendum_announcement.name: Referendum Announcement
crs_alg_referendum_announcement.text: The colonial center offers managed transition under pressure.

res_alg_urban_cell_network.name: Urban Cell Network
res_alg_urban_cell_network.text: Neighborhood cells move witness, shelter, and clandestine coordination across the capital.

res_alg_mountain_guerrilla_offensive.name: Mountain Guerrilla Offensive
res_alg_mountain_guerrilla_offensive.text: +2 on a campaign launched from the mountains or toward colonial army pressure.

res_alg_international_student_solidarity.name: International Student Solidarity
res_alg_international_student_solidarity.text: Student networks force the struggle into international discourse.

res_alg_labor_strike.name: Labor Strike
res_alg_labor_strike.text: Workers disrupt colonial commerce and settler confidence.

res_alg_cultural_resistance_campaign.name: Cultural Resistance Campaign
res_alg_cultural_resistance_campaign.text: +1 on campaigns that preserve memory, witness, and political legitimacy.

res_alg_underground_printing_press.name: Underground Printing Press
res_alg_underground_printing_press.text: Pamphlets, testimony, and clandestine news circulate despite censorship.

res_alg_mass_funeral_mobilization.name: Mass Funeral Mobilization
res_alg_mass_funeral_mobilization.text: Collective mourning strengthens determination and shared purpose.

res_alg_negotiation_delegation.name: Negotiation Delegation
res_alg_negotiation_delegation.text: Movement delegates force the question of colonial abuse into formal forums.

res_alg_cross_border_supply.name: Cross-Border Supply
res_alg_cross_border_supply.text: Border routes keep the movement materially alive across the eastern frontier.

res_alg_prison_solidarity_network.name: Prison Solidarity Network
res_alg_prison_solidarity_network.text: Families and clandestine networks keep prisoners politically present.

res_alg_ceasefire_proposal.name: Ceasefire Proposal
res_alg_ceasefire_proposal.text: +1 on campaigns that reduce colonial impunity or open negotiation space.

res_alg_grassroots_education.name: Grassroots Education
res_alg_grassroots_education.text: Political education binds scattered struggle into collective strategy.

sys_alg_colonial_army_sweep.name: Colonial Army Sweep
sys_alg_colonial_army_sweep.text: The colonial army tightens military pressure across the most vulnerable front.

sys_alg_settler_political_veto.name: Settler Political Veto
sys_alg_settler_political_veto.text: Settler pressure hardens both the colonial center and the metropole against compromise.

sys_alg_detention_grid_tightens.name: Detention Grid Tightens
sys_alg_detention_grid_tightens.text: The detention network widens to disappear militants and witnesses.

sys_alg_nato_cover.name: NATO Cover
sys_alg_nato_cover.text: Geopolitical backing shields colonial violence from immediate consequence.

sys_alg_counter_insurgency_doctrine.name: Counter-Insurgency Doctrine
sys_alg_counter_insurgency_doctrine.text: Doctrine spreads forced relocation, intelligence fusion, and punitive raids.

sys_alg_administrative_partition.name: Administrative Partition
sys_alg_administrative_partition.text: Fragmentation is used to isolate fronts and discipline the struggle.

res_corr_egyptian_medic_convoy.name: Egyptian Medic Convoy
res_corr_egyptian_medic_convoy.text: A narrow convoy window gets wounded people, medicine, and witness lists through before the gate is sealed again.

res_corr_qatari_mediation_channel.name: Qatari Mediation Channel
res_corr_qatari_mediation_channel.text: A mediation pause moves detainee names, casualty lists, and negotiation demands before the bombing cycle resumes.

res_corr_turkish_port_delay.name: Turkish Port Delay
res_corr_turkish_port_delay.text: Customs friction and port delay slow military cargo long enough to document routes and buy time.

res_corr_jordanian_hospital_witness.name: Jordanian Hospital Witness
res_corr_jordanian_hospital_witness.text: Wounded evacuees and hospital records cross the border, forcing massacre evidence back into public view.

res_corr_venezuelan_communal_kitchens.name: Venezuelan Communal Kitchens
res_corr_venezuelan_communal_kitchens.text: Communal kitchens blunt sanctions hunger and keep neighborhoods politically present.

res_corr_cuban_radio_brigade.name: Cuban Radio Brigade
res_corr_cuban_radio_brigade.text: Neighborhood radio carries memory, alerts, and anti-blockade coordination when platforms and banks choke movement.

res_corr_street_to_strait_coordination.name: Street-to-Strait Coordination
res_corr_street_to_strait_coordination.text: Dockworkers, medics, communes, and witness networks line up one brief corridor that delays war logistics and opens organizing space.

sup_corr_spain_breaks_ranks.name: Spain Breaks Ranks
sup_corr_spain_breaks_ranks.text: Acting faction gets +2 on a War Machine campaign. Madrid's public refusal punctures Atlantic unanimity and gives anti-war organizers usable political cover.

sup_corr_gaza_archive_stream.name: Gaza Archive Stream
sup_corr_gaza_archive_stream.text: Acting faction gets +2 on a Witness Corridors campaign. Phones, martyr lists, clinic logs, and burial records keep evidence moving against erasure.

sup_corr_prisoner_letter_chain.name: Prisoner Letter Chain
sup_corr_prisoner_letter_chain.text: Acting faction gets +2 on a Sanctions Breach campaign. Letters from prison turn disappearance into public testimony and weaken the state's monopoly on narrative.

sup_corr_red_sea_dock_strike.name: Red Sea Dock Strike
sup_corr_red_sea_dock_strike.text: Acting faction gets +2 on a Chokepoint Breakage campaign. A port stoppage turns delay into cost for shippers, insurers, and war logistics.

sup_corr_black_sea_grain_solidarity.name: Black Sea Grain Solidarity
sup_corr_black_sea_grain_solidarity.text: Acting faction gets +2 on a Bread Under Siege campaign. Grain routes and convoy information are reclaimed for food survival instead of war planning.

sup_corr_cuban_memory_brigade.name: Cuban Memory Brigade
sup_corr_cuban_memory_brigade.text: Acting faction gets +2 on a Witness Corridors campaign. Songs, classrooms, and neighborhood memory keep blockade trauma politically legible and hard to erase.

sup_corr_communal_defense_assembly.name: Communal Defense Assembly
sup_corr_communal_defense_assembly.text: Acting faction gets +2 on a Bread Under Siege campaign. An assembly redistributes risk, restores discipline, and turns survival into coordinated strategy.

crs_corr_rafah_gate_choked.name: Rafah Gate Choked Again
crs_corr_rafah_gate_choked.text: Crossing restrictions and inspection delays trap the wounded, squeeze medicine, and close another exit from Gaza.

crs_corr_west_bank_mass_raids.name: West Bank Mass Raids
crs_corr_west_bank_mass_raids.text: Night raids, checkpoints, and prison sweeps widen the siege and push testimony back under armed control.

crs_corr_beirut_suburbs_hit.name: Beirut Suburbs Hit
crs_corr_beirut_suburbs_hit.text: Bombardment drives displacement through Beirut's southern suburbs and widens the northern front.

crs_corr_ansar_allah_shipping_shock.name: Ansar Allah Shipping Shock
crs_corr_ansar_allah_shipping_shock.text: Missile risk and naval retaliation scramble shipping schedules, reroute cargo, and raise attention to the corridor war.

crs_corr_suez_queue_stalls.name: Suez Queue Stalls
crs_corr_suez_queue_stalls.text: Canal delays stack containers, slow grain, and sharpen food pressure far beyond the canal itself.

crs_corr_hormuz_insurance_panic.name: Hormuz Insurance Panic
crs_corr_hormuz_insurance_panic.text: Insurers reprice passage, tankers hesitate, and Gulf households are dragged deeper into war exposure.

crs_corr_gulf_base_alarm.name: Gulf Base Alarm
crs_corr_gulf_base_alarm.text: Retaliatory fire puts host-state bases on alert and spreads fear through workers, ports, and civilian infrastructure.

crs_corr_cuba_banking_freeze.name: Cuba Banking Freeze
crs_corr_cuba_banking_freeze.text: Banking restrictions freeze payments for medicine, shipping, and basic imports.

crs_corr_venezuela_oil_waiver_reversed.name: Venezuela Oil Waiver Reversed
crs_corr_venezuela_oil_waiver_reversed.text: Waiver reversal tightens fuel pressure, foreign-exchange pressure, and sanctions discipline on daily life.

crs_corr_black_sea_grain_fire.name: Black Sea Grain Fire
crs_corr_black_sea_grain_fire.text: Port strikes burn grain capacity and fold another front into hunger politics.

crs_corr_karoline_leavitt_press_discipli.name: Karoline Leavitt Press Discipline
crs_corr_karoline_leavitt_press_discipli.text: Official talking points narrow what counts as credible witness and push Gaza testimony off the agenda.

crs_corr_monroe_message_to_caracas.name: Monroe Message to Caracas and Havana
crs_corr_monroe_message_to_caracas.text: Raise War Machine by 1, add 1 Extraction to Caribbean Siege, and lower Witness Corridors by 1. Threats to Caracas and Havana harden the blockade line and warn the hemisphere that disobedient sovereignty will be punished.

sys_corr_trump_operation_epic_fury.name: Trump Orders Operation Epic Fury
sys_corr_trump_operation_epic_fury.text: Direct U.S. strikes widen the war, tighten Gaza and Hormuz, and make every coalition campaign harder to land.

sys_corr_hegseth_forward_posture.name: Pete Hegseth Announces Forward Posture
sys_corr_hegseth_forward_posture.text: Forward-posture rhetoric normalizes more basing, more carriers, and a higher cost for stopping escalation.

sys_corr_ratcliffe_threat_brief.name: Ratcliffe Threat Brief
sys_corr_ratcliffe_threat_brief.text: Threat inflation turns intelligence into escalation cover and makes de-escalation politically harder to defend.

sys_corr_centcom_basing_web.name: U.S. Central Command Basing Web
sys_corr_centcom_basing_web.text: The Gulf basing lattice keeps sorties, refueling, and coercive logistics moving across the region.

sys_corr_ofac_secondary_squeeze.name: OFAC Secondary Squeeze
sys_corr_ofac_secondary_squeeze.text: Secondary sanctions spread fear through banks and insurers, making Caribbean trade, medicine, and outreach harder.

sys_corr_doj_extraterritorial_casework.name: DOJ Extraterritorial Casework
sys_corr_doj_extraterritorial_casework.text: Casework and sealed dockets turn sanctions into daily legal siege for Cuba- and Venezuela-linked networks.

sys_corr_netanyahu_widens_front.name: Netanyahu Widens the Front
sys_corr_netanyahu_widens_front.text: The war-state expands bombardment and displacement across Gaza and Lebanon rather than let the fronts separate.

sys_corr_israel_katz_corridor_threats.name: Israel Katz Corridor Threats
sys_corr_israel_katz_corridor_threats.text: Expulsion threats and corridor-control language shrink civilian space and punish any opening at Rafah.

sys_corr_uk_atlantic_alignment.name: UK Atlantic Alignment
sys_corr_uk_atlantic_alignment.text: London's alignment lends diplomatic cover to escalation and hardens the wider war atmosphere.

sys_corr_palantir_target_stack.name: Palantir Target Stack
sys_corr_palantir_target_stack.text: Data fusion compresses the path from surveillance to strike and makes witness networks easier to hunt.

sys_corr_murdoch_bollore_war_line.name: Murdoch and Bollore Set the War Line
sys_corr_murdoch_bollore_war_line.text: Broadcast empires narrow public language, delegitimize anti-war speech, and lower the cost of further escalation.

sys_corr_meta_visibility_drop.name: Meta Visibility Drop
sys_corr_meta_visibility_drop.text: Platform throttling buries testimony, slows organizing, and makes witness harder to circulate.

sys_corr_blackrock_risk_pricing.name: BlackRock Risk Pricing
sys_corr_blackrock_risk_pricing.text: Finance treats war as volatility to price rather than ruin to stop, tightening extraction around the Gulf.

sys_corr_trafigura_glencore_insurance_su.name: Trafigura / Glencore Insurance Surge
sys_corr_trafigura_glencore_insurance_su.text: Commodity traders pass maritime panic straight into fuel and food costs across corridor economies.

tahrir_square.name: 2011 — Tahrir Square
tahrir_square.description: For eighteen days, we were free. Then the struggle widened.
tahrir_square.introduction: Lead January 25 as a movement struggle against a security state that keeps reorganizing.
tahrir_square.story: The uprising spills from Tahrir into factories, neighborhoods, and detention cells. Every concession from above is contested below; every opening is met by a reorganized apparatus.
tahrir_square.dramatization: Tear gas, chants, funerals, and barricades. The square fills, empties, and fills again while the System searches for a decisive break.
tahrir_square.gameplay: Keep Cairo politically alive while managing nationwide pressure. If The Square empties too long, Revolutionary Wave recedes; martyrdom can restore momentum, but at severe human cost.
tahrir_square.moralCenter: No single hero saves this table. Collective endurance keeps the uprising alive, even when victory remains partial.

the_square.label: The Square
the_square.description: If Cairo is empty of comrades for two consecutive rounds, Revolutionary Wave decreases by 1.

martyrdom.label: Martyrdom
martyrdom.description: For every 4 comrades removed from Cairo, Revolutionary Wave increases by 1.

woman_life_freedom.name: 2022 — Woman, Life, Freedom
woman_life_freedom.description: Woman, Life, Freedom under patriarchal rule, censorship, and force.
woman_life_freedom.introduction: Lead a women-fronted uprising against patriarchal and militarized control.
woman_life_freedom.story: After Jina (Mahsa) Amini's killing, revolt spreads across streets, campuses, and marginalized regions. The movement grows through testimony, memory, and refusal while the System escalates repression.
woman_life_freedom.dramatization: Headscarves burn, classrooms become organizing cells, and prison walls answer with silence and fear. The chant keeps moving anyway.
woman_life_freedom.gameplay: Lower Patriarchal Grip while sustaining witness networks under heavy crackdown. Symbolic gains matter, but they collapse without structural pressure management.
woman_life_freedom.moralCenter: Women's liberation is collective liberation. Every gain is real, and none arrives without risk.

hijab_enforcement.label: Hijab Enforcement
hijab_enforcement.description: High enforcement regions penalize woman-led movement and organize actions.

gender_dynamics.label: Gender Dynamics
gender_dynamics.description: Male allies can shield or transfer actions to support female-led factions.

algerian_war_of_independence.name: 1954 — Algerian War of Independence
algerian_war_of_independence.introduction: Lead a movement-centered anti-colonial struggle against French militarism, detention, and settler veto between 1954 and 1962.
algerian_war_of_independence.story: Organizing survives across Algiers, Kabylia, the settler coast, the Sahara, border corridors, and the metropole. Every breakthrough triggers retaliation; every act of witness invites another attempt at erasure.
algerian_war_of_independence.dramatization: Night raids, clandestine presses, intercepted routes, mass funerals, and the persistent knowledge that liberation will not erase the violence already lived.
algerian_war_of_independence.gameplay: Evidence builds international witness but raises Repression Cycle. Urban gains can raise War Machine. Symbolic victory requires acknowledgement of colonial abuse, not attention alone.
algerian_war_of_independence.moralCenter: Liberation is collective and costly. Ending colonial rule does not end grief, but it changes who decides the future.

repression_cycle.name: Repression Cycle
repression_cycle.description: How far colonial repression has escalated against the movement.

repression_feedback.label: Repression Feedback
repression_feedback.description: Whenever the coalition gains Evidence, Repression Cycle rises by 1.

urban_operations.label: Urban Operations
urban_operations.description: Successful campaigns in Algiers or Oran increase War Machine by 1.

referendum_pressure.label: Force Immediate Referendum
referendum_pressure.description: Symbolic victory requires international acknowledgement of colonial abuse, not just attention.

repression_cycle.label: Repression Cycle
repression_cycle.description: At 5 the movement suffers mass detention. At 7 torture is exposed. At 9 a nationwide state of emergency hardens every campaign. At 10 the coalition loses 1 Comrade each round.

egypt_1919_revolution.name: 1919 — Egypt Rises
egypt_1919_revolution.introduction: The 1919 uprising is a mass coalition struggle. Students, railway workers, and women’s Action Circles must hold Cairo and the provinces while British occupation attempts to suppress popular mobilization through detention and martial law.
egypt_1919_revolution.story: The uprising spills from Cairo's universities and the Delta's rail lines into every village and port. Every strike in Alexandria is met by a blockade in the south; every demonstration in the capital is echoed in the Suez Canal Zone.
egypt_1919_revolution.dramatization: British bayonets meet student strikes at Al-Azhar. Women march against the exile of their leaders. The railway lines are cut, and for a few weeks, the colonial machine cannot move.
egypt_1919_revolution.gameplay: Organize strikes and sabotage logistics corridors while holding back mass arrests in Cairo. Symbolic victory requires the full mobilization of provincial networks and the public defiance of colonial constraints.
egypt_1919_revolution.moralCenter: Collective defiance is the only shield. When the whole nation rises, the empire is forced to blink.

railway_sabotage.label: Railway Sabotage
railway_sabotage.description: Successful campaigns in Delta Rail Corridor reduce System Escalation speed.

funerary_mobilization.label: Funerary Mobilization
funerary_mobilization.description: Losing Comrades in urban zones adds +1 to Martyrs’ Reckoning progress.

RevolutionaryWave.name: National Uprising
RevolutionaryWave.description: The momentum of mass defiance across urban centers and villages.

SilencedTruth.name: Public Testimony
SilencedTruth.description: The ability to speak colonial crimes to the world and coordinate the movement.

EmptyStomach.name: Strike Capacity
EmptyStomach.description: Economic disruption through labor refusal and civil disobedience.

GildedCage.name: Anti-Detention Pressure
GildedCage.description: The demand for the return of exiled leaders and the release of political detainees.

StolenVoice.name: Mass Representation
StolenVoice.description: Pluralist political representation that outgrows elite negotiation.

UnfinishedJustice.name: Martyrs’ Reckoning
UnfinishedJustice.description: The collective memory of those fallen, fueling continued resistance.

Cairo.description: The administrative heart and center of the student uprising and women’s demonstrations.

Alexandria.description: Port city with fierce labor presence and dockworker strikes against colonial logistics.

UpperEgypt.description: Autonomous districts and Assiut’s defiance, where state control is fragile.

when_the_corridors_burn.name: 2026 — When the Corridors Burn
when_the_corridors_burn.description: A corridor-war scenario where crossings, ports, banks, and hospitals become weapons against the people who need them.
when_the_corridors_burn.introduction: Lead a five-seat coalition through a connected war of siege, sanctions, shipping disruption, basing pressure, and public erasure.
when_the_corridors_burn.story: What is called escalation is a corridor order that turns crossings, ports, hospitals, banks, and grain routes into pressure points. Gaza remains the moral center while Lebanon burns wider, Hormuz and Suez choke, and the blockade line stretches toward Cuba and Venezuela.
when_the_corridors_burn.dramatization: Hospitals count the wounded and the missing, dockworkers count delays, communes count remaining fuel, and every new missile path threatens another population already living under discipline.
when_the_corridors_burn.gameplay: Hold the fronts where people are being trapped, displaced, or starved, then fracture the shipping, sanctions, and basing system before the War Machine outruns the coalition's witness and survival networks.
when_the_corridors_burn.moralCenter: No northern savior arrives. The table lives or dies by movement endurance, witness, and survival structures built by the people under attack.

WarMachine.name: War Machine Fracture
WarMachine.description: Breaking basing chains, arms corridors, and the political tempo of escalation.

SilencedTruth.name: Witness Corridors
SilencedTruth.description: Keeping testimony, archives, medics, and public proof in motion under bombardment and blackout.

EmptyStomach.name: Bread Under Siege
EmptyStomach.description: Holding food and daily survival against siege, sanctions, and manipulated scarcity.

GildedCage.name: Sanctions Breach
GildedCage.description: Cracking carceral, financial, and legal siege across borders and banking systems.

FossilGrip.name: Chokepoint Breakage
FossilGrip.description: Interrupting the oil-shipping-basing architecture that treats seas and straits as extractive property.

egypt_corridor.name: Egyptian Corridor Posture
egypt_corridor.description: 0-6 track: from sealed containment to pressured opening and mediated passage.

turkey_corridor.name: Turkish Corridor Posture
turkey_corridor.description: 0-6 track: from NATO contradiction under management to more visible rupture and corridor pressure.

jordan_corridor.name: Jordanian Corridor Posture
jordan_corridor.description: 0-6 track: from containment priority to fragile opening under Palestine-adjacent pressure.

qatar_corridor.name: Qatari Mediation Posture
qatar_corridor.description: 0-6 track: mediation capacity under pressure, useful when witness and negotiation both still move.

gulf_blowback.name: Gulf Blowback
gulf_blowback.description: Shared host-state exposure to retaliatory fire, insurance panic, and basing instability.

regional_powers_layer.label: Regional Powers / Corridor States
regional_powers_layer.description: Egypt, Turkey, Jordan, and Qatar use individual posture tracks while the Gulf hosts sit inside a shared blowback subsystem.

gulf_host_target_subsystem.label: Host-and-Target Gulf Subsystem
gulf_host_target_subsystem.description: Saudi Arabia, UAE, Bahrain, and Kuwait are not one camp. They hedge, host, or distance under one shared Gulf Blowback meter.

atlantic_fracture.label: Atlantic Fracture
atlantic_fracture.description: Spain can widen anti-war space while the UK can harden Atlantic alignment and raise campaign pressure.

corridor_war_not_state_war.label: Corridor War
corridor_war_not_state_war.description: Shipping, food, basing, sanctions, and testimony are all fronts. A passage opened elsewhere can still save Gaza, Cuba, or Venezuela.
